data.title: Saudi Arabia's ACWA Power to buy Engie's share in Kuwait and Bahrain assets
data.description:
Saudi Arabia's ACWA Power plans to buy stakes in assets in Kuwait and Bahrain worth $693 million from French utility developer ENGIE , according to a joint statement on Wednesday.

The share purchase agreement covers operating capacities of 4.61 GW of gas-fired power generation and 1.11 million cubic meters per day (m3/day) of water desalination facilities, the companies said in a statement.

It also includes the related operations and maintenance companies in Kuwait and Bahrain, it added.

Completion of the transaction is subject to customary regulatory and other stakeholder approvals.

data.title: Integrum Seeks SEK31 Million from Share Issuance
data.description:
Integrum (INTEG-B.ST) said Tuesday that it aims to raise 31 million kronor from the issuance of 1,937,500 B shares at 16 kronor apiece.

The Swedish medical technology company earmarked net proceeds from the share sale for the growth of its sales force in the US, research and development initiatives, and as working capital.

Carnegie Investment Bank acted as the sole bookrunner.

data.title: SAL Saudi Logistics Services' FY24 Net Profit, Revenue Rise
data.description:
SAL Saudi Logistics Services (SASE:4263) said Wednesday its net profit and revenue for the full year 2024 climbed year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 661.4 million Saudi riyals, compared with 509.7 million riyals a year ago. EPS moved to 8.27 riyals from 6.37 riyals.

The Saudi Arabia-listed logistics company's revenue was 1.63 billion riyals, compared with 1.46 billion riyals earlier.

Meanwhile, the board declared a cash dividend of 1.33 riyals per share for the fourth quarter, payable within 15 days of the eligibility date of Feb. 26. It paid 1.51 riyals per share a year ago.

data.title: Will Semiconductor to Gain as More Chinese EVs Get Driving Assistance — Market Talk
data.description: Will Semiconductor is well-placed to benefit from the increased prevalence of advanced driver-assistance systems in Chinese vehicles, Citi analysts say. The Chinese semiconductor company's OX08D automotive image sensor is used by carmakers like BYD, which recently started rolling out driver-assistance features on a significant portion of its models, they say in a note. Will Semiconductor could also be poised for further re-rating on the back of China's growing emphasis on supply localization, thanks to its leadership in both the auto and smartphone sector, they add. Citi maintains a buy rating on the stock and raises the target price to CNY180.00 from CNY135.00. Shares last at CNY153.90.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: German Elections Back Deutsche Bank Research's Positive View on Local, European Equities
data.description:
Deutsche Bank Research said the upcoming elections in Germany are largely positive for equities at home and the wider European region.

Analysts expect a new government to result in one or a combination of higher fiscal spending, tax cuts and lower energy costs, which are all upside catalysts for markets.

"As the year-to-date rally in European Equities is already reflecting better sentiment, we expect little short-term implications for the DAX and expect the MDAX to be the bigger long-term beneficiary in case of effective reforms," the firm wrote.

On the other hand, fragmentation, long coalition negotiations and a blocking minority of the far-right AfD may weaken market enthusiasm for the potential reforms.

Voters in Germany, the biggest economy in the eurozone, will elect a new government on Feb. 23.

data.title: Market Chatter: New World's State Pavilia Project's Latest Apartments Nearly 100 Times Oversubscribed
data.description:
New World Development project State Pavilia obtained more than 8,450 checks for 88 apartments for sale on Wednesday, The Standard reported the same day.

The apartments are nearly 100 times oversubscribed, a new record for new projects by New World, the report said.

State Pavilia's latest batch of apartments are priced at HK$19,866 per square foot after discounts, 5% higher than the first, the report said.

Among the apartments on offer are 29 one-bedroom units and 59 two-bedroom units sized between 343 and 621 square feet, The Standard said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Rio Tinto Group Says Mitsui to Pick Up 40% Stake in Western Australia Iron Ore Project Joint Venture
data.description:
Rio Tinto Group welcomed Mitsui to the Rhodes Ridge joint venture for the Rhodes Ridge iron ore project in Western Australia after the Japanese firm entered agreements to acquire a 40% stake in the venture from its partners, according to a Wednesday release from the mining firm.

The Japanese firm agreed to acquire all of VOC Group's 25% stake in the joint venture. It also entered into a heads of agreement to acquire a further 15% stake in the venture from AMB Holdings. AMB Holdings will retain a 10% stake in the venture.

The deals are subject to regulatory approvals and other closing conditions.

Wright Prospecting's former 50% interest in the joint venture was restructured and separated into two newly formed entities, separately controlled by its shareholders, VOC Group and AMB Holdings.

Rio Tinto's 50% stake and the terms of the joint venture remain unchanged.

First ore from the project is expected by 2030.

data.title: Yamama Cement Logs Higher FY24 Net Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Yamama Cement (SASE:3020) said Wednesday that its net profit and revenue for the full-year 2024 jumped year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 420.7 million Saudi riyals, compared with 304.3 million riyals a year ago. EPS moved to 2.08 riyals from 1.5 riyals.

The Saudi Arabia-listed cement manufacturer's revenue was 1.17 billion riyals, compared with 935.2 million riyals earlier.

Meanwhile, the board declared an unchanged cash dividend of 1 riyal per share for the year. The dividend's payment date is subject to approval at a general assembly.

data.title: Market Chatter: Hong Kong Property Investor Divesting Villas for HK$2 Billion
data.description:
Hong Kong property investment company Gale Well Group will sell Jade Beach Villa at Chung Hom Kok in the city for HK$2 billion, The Standard reported Wednesday.

The developer will put up the villas for sale to reduce its debts amid high interest rates, the report said.

Gale Well Chief Executive Officer Jacinto Tong Men-leung said the debt reduction is part of the normal course of business, the report said.

Tong, however, did not disclose how much the company is in debt, The Standard said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: BSP Financial Group Posts Higher 2024 Earnings, Revenue
data.description:
BSP Financial Group reported Wednesday fiscal 2024 earnings of 2.221 Papua New Guinea kina per diluted share, up from 1.905 kina per diluted share a year earlier.

Revenue for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 2.98 billion kina, rising 8% compared with 2.75 billion kina a year earlier.

The board declared a final dividend of 1.21 kina per share, up from 1.06 kina in the previously reported period, payable March 21 to shareholders on record as of Feb. 25.

data.title: Invesco Asia Trust Rebrands as Invesco Asia Dragon Trust After Merger
data.description:
Invesco Asia Trust (IAT.L) said Tuesday that it formally changed its name to Invesco Asia Dragon Trust on Monday.

The rebranding is in line with the investment company's merger with Asia Dragon Trust. Accordingly, its ticker on the London stock exchange was changed to IAD from IAT, with effect from Feb. 14.

The name change will formally be reflected on the bourse on Wednesday.

data.title: Soccer-Arsenal defender Tomiyasu undergoes knee surgery
data.description:
Takehiro Tomiyasu has become the latest Arsenal player to go under the knife after the Japanese defender said on Tuesday he had undergone surgery on his right knee.

The 26-year-old did not mention an expected return date though British media reported he would likely be sidelined for the remainder of the season.

Tomiyasu is the third Arsenal player to have surgery over the past month following Kai Havertz and Gabriel Jesus.

"I've had a surgery on my knee few days ago and I've already started my rehab to do what I love the most again." Tomiyasu wrote on Instagram.

"It has been the toughest period in my career for sure and it carries on a bit more but I won't give up."

The Japan international, who joined Arsenal from Bologna in 2021, made 30 appearances last season but injury has limited him to playing just six minutes in the current campaign, coming on as a substitute against Southampton in a league game in October.

Arsenal, who trail Premier League leaders Liverpool by seven points, host West Ham United on Saturday.
